description:
The github-issues plugin (lives in /home/mark/dev/balls-github-plugin, crate balls-plugin-github-issues) falsely flips healthy tasks to status=deferred. On 2026-06-07 it deferred 5 live tasks (bl-72a8 the greenfield EPIC, bl-b5c0, bl-d7a5, bl-0802, bl-37b1), confusing agents. Plugin disabled in .balls/config.json; tasks manually restored.

ROOT CAUSE: src/pull.rs::list_issues does GET /repos/{o}/{n}/issues?state=all with NO per_page and NO pagination. Doc-comment admits it: "Pull pagination is intentionally not handled in B4a (one page = whatever GH defaults to, 30) ... Pagination is a future concern." GH defaults to 30/page. mudbungie/balls has 331 issues, so only the ~30 most-recent are listed.

CONSEQUENCE: src/pull_emit.rs::sweep_deletes builds known_numbers from that ~30-issue page; any mirrored task whose stored issue_number is off-page-1 hits deleted_from(), which under on_external_delete=deferred flips it to status=deferred with note "GH issue #N no longer found in repo". VERIFIED FALSE: gh issue view 307 -R mudbungie/balls -> OPEN. sweep_deletes comment names "a pagination-induced false-positive" as known but only bounds it (MAX_DELETES_PER_SYNC=500).

FIX: paginate list_issues (Link rel=next, or page loop with per_page=100) so known_numbers is COMPLETE before the delete-sweep; never treat a partial/failed listing as authoritative for deletion.

REPRO: any repo >30 issues; bl sync; off-page-1 tasks flip to deferred.

issue.last_synced_title: github-issues plugin: unpaginated list_issues (page 1 of 30) makes sweep_deletes falsely defer tasks [bl-bb66]

title: github-issues plugin: unpaginated list_issues (page 1 of 30) makes sweep_deletes falsely defer tasks
